channel.title: Optimization on RadixArk Blog
channel.description: Recent content in Optimization on RadixArk Blog

item.title: The Calculus of Gradient Descent
item.description:
Training a model is simply finding the minimum of a high-dimensional surface. To get there, we follow the slope downwards.

The Objective

Given a cost function \( J(\theta) \), we want to find the parameters \( \theta \) that minimize \( J \). The update rule for simple gradient descent is:

$$ \theta_{t+1} = \theta_t - \eta \nabla J(\theta_t) $$

Where \( \eta \) is the learning rate.

Momentum and Adam

Standard gradient descent oscillates in ravines. Adding a momentum term \( v_t \) dampens this:
